root_text.sn22.126:1.2: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.126:2.1: “Idha, bhikkhu, assutavā puthujjano samudayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ ‘samudayadhammaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti;
root_text.sn22.126:3.1: Evaṁ vutte, so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.126:4.1: “Idha, bhikkhu, sutavā ariyasāvako samudayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ ‘samudayadhammaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti;

translation_text.sn22.126:1.2: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn22.126:1.3: “Sir, they speak of this thing called ‘ignorance’.
translation_text.sn22.126:1.5: And how is an ignorant person defined?”
translation_text.sn22.126:2.1: “Mendicant, it’s when an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t truly understand form, which is liable to originate, as form which is liable to originate.
translation_text.sn22.126:2.2: They don’t truly understand form, which is liable to fall, as form which is liable to fall.
translation_text.sn22.126:2.3: They don’t truly understand form, which is liable to originate and fall, as form which is liable to originate and fall.
translation_text.sn22.126:2.11: consciousness, which is liable to originate, as consciousness which is liable to originate.
translation_text.sn22.126:2.12: They don’t truly understand consciousness, which is liable to fall, as consciousness which is liable to fall.
translation_text.sn22.126:2.13: They don’t truly understand consciousness, which is liable to originate and fall, as consciousness which is liable to originate and fall.
translation_text.sn22.126:2.15: And this is how an ignorant person is defined.”
translation_text.sn22.126:3.1: When he said this, the mendicant said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn22.126:3.2: “Sir, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’.
translation_text.sn22.126:3.4: And how is a knowledgeable person defined?”
translation_text.sn22.126:4.1: “Mendicant, it’s when a learned noble disciple truly understands form, which is liable to originate, as form which is liable to originate.
translation_text.sn22.126:4.2: They truly understand form, which is liable to fall, as form which is liable to fall.
translation_text.sn22.126:4.3: They truly understand form, which is liable to originate and vanish, as form which is liable to originate and vanish.
translation_text.sn22.126:4.11: consciousness, which is liable to originate, as consciousness which is liable to originate.
translation_text.sn22.126:4.12: They truly understand consciousness, which is liable to fall, as consciousness which is liable to fall.
translation_text.sn22.126:4.13: They truly understand consciousness, which is liable to originate and fall, as consciousness which is liable to originate and fall.
translation_text.sn22.126:4.15: And this is how a knowledgeable person is defined.”
